title: Eyleen
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Eyleen.jpg]] |name=Eyleen |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Legacy|Repo Man|Tatooine}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blonde]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I have found the ones that bought the booster controller for the droid's motivator, but I warn you: they are strange ones.''"
"''I've met plenty of 'strange ones' already.|[[Watto/Legends|Watto]] and a spacer|}} '''Eyleen''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who lived on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], she was part of a group of thugs[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Complete Guide: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: The Complete Guide'': Prima Official Game Guide]] who lived in the slums of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]]. This group also included [[Hilbrun]], [[Tiomvo]] and [[Guz'Ordnak]]. In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],{{SWG_timeline}} they bought the booster controller of an [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2 unit]] [[Motivator/Legends|motivator]] to the [[Junk dealer/Legends|junk dealer]] [[Watto/Legends|Watto]]. One of them left his [[Swoop/Legends|swoop bike]] as collateral, but they never paid Watto for the booster controller. Since this R2 unit was wanted by both [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Arlan Barrezz|Barrezz]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Carh'la Bastra|Bastra]], a spacer later tried to recover the motivator booster controller. Eyleen and the thieves attacked the spacer on sight, but the spacer managed to retrieve the booster controller from Hilbrun's [[USV-5 Modified Landspeeder]]. ==Behind the scenes== Eyleen was a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]]. The game was shut down on [[December 15]], [[2011]].{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}} Eyleen was added to the game as part of the "[[W:c:swg:Legacy Quest|Legacy Quest]]" with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]].{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}} [[File:DaeziiMae.png|thumb|right|100px|Eyleen, ready for action]] Eyleen is an enemy NPC who might attack the player when the player tries to retrieve the engine parts from a landspeeder situated right next to her. When she attacks, the player will have to kill her in order to get the parts, as one cannot interact with objects while in combat mode. However, since it is possible to sneak around her and retrieve the parts unnoticed, her death or survival is not canonically established. According to the game's directory .tree files and reference manuals, this character was originally named '''Daezii Mae'''. ==Appearances== *{{GalaxiesNGE}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Complete Guide: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: The Complete Guide'': Prima Official Game Guide]] {{ID|Daezii Mae}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Eyleen}} {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{SWGWiki|Repo Man}} [[Category:Criminals]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Tatooinians]]
